test.observation: Jest ran 45 suites / 400 tests and all passed. Output still included the existing agent-store/agent-init console logging, the wallet-connect error-path console.error, and Jest's usual open-handle warning after completion.

typecheck.observation: tsc --noEmit completed successfully with no TypeScript diagnostics.

lint.observation: ESLint exited 0 but reported one `no-void` warning at `src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts:498`.

blockingIssues.description: `restoreFromMnemonic()` still only calls `agent.initialize({ recoveryPhrase })` before scheduling `refreshIdentities()`. Because upstream assigns `agentDid` during `agent.start()` rather than `initialize()`, the new retry poller never observes readiness in RecoveryRestore flows and can still leave restored wallets with stale or empty identities until a later manual refresh or relaunch.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten the handoff honesty gate so workers do not claim `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` unless the transcript shows the required context/environment reads and a genuine red-first test cycle (or an explicit recorded deviation).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `misc-suppress-agent-did-race-warnings` and `fix-agent-did-race-retry-when-ready` marked `followedProcedure: true` even though their transcript skeletons missed required environment/context reads and show code edits before the new retry-focused test file existed.

rejectedObservations.observation: The mission shared state does not document the crucial upstream asymmetry that `EnboxUserAgent.initialize()` leaves `agentDid` unset while `start()` assigns it, so workers have to rediscover it from `node_modules` when touching agent/store code.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in `.factory/library/architecture.md:249-253` during misc-3 scrutiny round 1
